commit.message:
fix(jetson): resolve critical CUDA threading, crash bugs, and edge cases

C1+C2: Replace direct TensorRTInference with InferenceWorkerPool in
gui_app.py — engines now load on dedicated CUDA thread, eliminating
context-affinity violations and shared-buffer race conditions.

C3: Fix TypeError crash in provision.py when device_id is int
(str(device_id)[:8] instead of device_id[:8]).

C4: Remove conflicting WatchdogSec=120 from static agrikd.service
template (setup_jetson.sh generates the actual Docker-based service).

H1: Defer gui_app.py file logging setup until after os.chdir() so
log files are created in the correct directory.

H2: Add basic config validation in gui_app.py main().

H3: Wrap sync_engine.py resp.json() in try/except to prevent crash
on non-JSON responses (e.g., proxy error pages).

H4: Add max_retries=5 to camera._open_with_reconnect() to prevent
infinite blocking during shutdown.

H5: Call pool.shutdown() in gui_app.py closeEvent to release CUDA
device memory on GUI close.

H7: Run health_server.py DB cleanup in background thread to avoid
blocking the Waitress request thread.

M9: Throttle gui_app.py cleanup to every 50 predictions (was every 1).
M10: Guard sync_engine.py device_id int cast with try/except.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

INSTRUCTIONS.md, *.instructions.md) are always exempt from the
source/misplaced-features rule — added in #426/#460 — but the
rule reference page did not mention this behaviour.

descriptions, and add correct/incorrect examples for instruction files
at the repo root.

commit.message:
End of internship - ASU Fall 2025 (#28)

* update libraries versions in both python and typescript, and several small bug fixes. Added separate startup scripts not requiring the electron app.

* feat: Unified startup system and improved architecture

## Infrastructure
- Add unified start.sh script for managing both local and Docker deployments
- Add docker-compose.yml for container orchestration
- Add Dockerfiles for frontend (Node 18) and backend (Python 3.13)
- Remove obsolete startup scripts (restart_app.sh, start_*.sh)
- Add /datasets/ directory and move dataset generation tools out of backend

## Backend Improvements
- Merge new semantic_comparison.py with enhanced error handling and flexible model loading
- Remove LLM comparison functionality (app/ai/llm_comparison.py)
- Add model management router (/app/routers/models.py) with select/delete/import/list endpoints
- Add /models/translation/* and /models/comparison/* API endpoints
- Fix semantic comparison default model to use full identifier 'sentence-transformers/LaBSE'
- Update comparison API to support new model management

## Frontend Improvements
- Remove all backend lifecycle management from main.ts (kill/start processes)
- Add health check IPC handler calling /health endpoint
- Update TranslationSection to use /health endpoint for status checks
- Remove startBackend function and 'Start Backend' button from UI
- Fix compareArticles service to send correct model name 'sentence-transformers/LaBSE'

## Testing
- Fix all 4 failing tests (mock patches and cache handling)
- Achieve 100% test pass rate (56/56 tests passing)
- Rename semantic_comparison_tests.py to test_semantic_comparison.py
- Convert to proper pytest format with test_* functions
- Fix mock data for translation tests (add 'fr' language support)

## Documentation
- Update README.md with comprehensive start.sh usage documentation
- Add Docker Compose workflow documentation
- Update API endpoints list (remove LLM comparison, add model management)
- Update test statistics (56 tests, 100% passing)
- Update backend and frontend README files
- Fix backend path references in frontend documentation

## Architecture
- Frontend now only monitors backend health, does not manage lifecycle
- Backend lifecycle managed exclusively by start.sh script
- Both local and Docker workflows use same start.sh interface
- Services are independent with proper separation of concerns

This consolidates 4 startup scripts into 1 unified system with full Docker support
and removes all LLM comparison code in favor of semantic comparison only.

commit.message:
fix: static block context isolation, continue target validation, object literal semicolons, break/continue ASI

- Static blocks: save/clear loop_stack and try_finally_stack in compiler so break/continue from outer loops don't leak through
- Continue statement: validate that labeled continue targets iteration statements, not arbitrary labeled blocks
- LoopContext: add is_iteration flag to distinguish iteration statements from labeled blocks/switches
- Object literal parser: stop skipping semicolons in property loop (semicolons are invalid in object literals)
- Break/continue ASI: skip LineTerminators before checking for semicolon terminator
- Static block parser: save/clear generator and function context, push method context for super access
- Arrow function body: clear forbidden_await_identifier (static block sets it, arrow body clears it)
- Class name await: check forbid_await_identifier without in_await_context exception for module mode
- Variable declaration: reject await as binding name when forbidden
- Computed property names in class: wrap with with_allowed_in to prevent FORBID_IN leak
- Method/constructor context: push before params for super in default parameter expressions
- Module mode: wrap parsing with forbidden_await_identifier
- Remove dead code: unused destructuring_element_scan_mask and params_scan_mask functions

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: GitHub poller hitting node + runtime limits on discovery (#14)

* fix(github): don't fetch CI for terminal PRs

* fix(tracker): cap repos per discovery query to avoid GitHub runtime limits

The "Resource limits for this query exceeded" error is a per-query
runtime cost ceiling, distinct from the 500k static node limit. A
search across 6 repos with a 30-day merged-PRs window and a rich
fragment exceeds it even with the lightweight discovery fragment,
because the search engine has to scan too many candidates per query.

Add maxReposPerQuery = 3 to scopedQueries so each search query
covers at most 3 repos instead of packing all 6 into one. With 6
repos this produces 2 scoped queries per base instead of 1 — 16
total discovery queries per poll instead of 8. Each query searches
half as many repos, returning roughly half the candidates, staying
well under the runtime budget.

Trade-off is more round trips, but each GraphQL search costs ~1
rate-limit point and we have a 5000/hour budget, so 16 queries per
minute-interval poll = 960/hour — plenty of headroom.

The fragment split in the previous commit (don't fetch CI for terminal
PRs) reduces per-PR node cost. This commit reduces per-query result
count. Together they address both the static node-budget ceiling and
the dynamic runtime-cost ceiling that were breaking the poller.

* fix(github): batch RefreshPRs to stay under per-query runtime limit

Same "Resource limits for this query exceeded" error, different path:
RefreshPRs sends all open-PR node IDs in a single nodes(ids: [...])
call with the full fragment. With enough open PRs, the per-query
runtime cost exceeds GitHub's ceiling even though the static node
budget is fine.

Add refreshBatchSize = 20 and split RefreshPRs into chunked calls
to refreshPRsBatch. Transparent to callers — same signature, same
return type. 20 IDs × ~1,060 nodes/PR = ~21k nodes per batch, well
under both the 500k static limit and the runtime budget.

With 100 open PRs: 5 batches × 20 = 5 round trips. Each is cheap
and the rate-limit overhead (~1 point per query) is negligible.

* fix(tracker): store discovery snapshot + remove dead err check

Two review fixes:

1. Phase 1 now stores the discovery snapshot via UpdateTrackedSnapshot
   immediately after UpsertTrackedItem. Without this, UpsertTrackedItem
   stored '{}' as the default snapshot, Phase 2 parsed that as a
   zero-value PRSnapshot (Merged=false, State=""), and every newly-
   discovered PR — including merged/closed backfill results — was
   classified as "open" and refreshed with the expensive full fragment.
   This undermined the entire fragment-split optimization and could
   reintroduce the runtime-limit failures on first-run/backfill.

   The discovery snapshot is overwritten by the refresh result in the
   same poll cycle (Phase 3 calls UpdateTrackedSnapshot with the real
   data), so the brief window where it holds the lightweight snapshot
   is harmless — it's only used for open/terminal classification.

2. Removed dead code at the Phase 3 boundary: the `if err != nil`
   check was left over from when Phase 3 had its own
   ListActiveTrackedItems call. After the refactor that moved the
   load into Phase 2, `err` at that point was the already-handled
   result from Phase 2 — the refresh calls shadow `err` inside their
   own if-blocks. The check could never fire and was misleading.

* feat(ui): show skipped checks as distinct color in checks bar

Skipped checks (e.g. conditional workflows that determined they don't
apply to this PR) were previously bucketed with passing, inflating
the "X/Y passing" count without being distinguishable. Now they get
their own category:

Backend (status.go):
- ChecksStatus gains a Skipped int field
- Counting logic adds a `cr.Conclusion == "skipped"` case between
  the failing and default (passing) branches

Frontend (PRCard.tsx):
- ChecksBar type gains skipped
- New bar segment in text-tertiary/20 (subtle gray) between passing
  (green) and pending (yellow) — visually present but muted, matching
  the "this ran but didn't apply" semantics
- Label shows "X/Y passing · Z skipped" when skipped > 0

* fix(tracker): only seed discovery snapshot for newly-inserted items

The previous commit (0c5b7b9) stored the discovery snapshot for every
discovered PR unconditionally via UpdateTrackedSnapshot. For already-
tracked PRs, this overwrote the previous cycle's full-refresh snapshot
(with real CheckRuns) with the lightweight discovery snapshot (nil
CheckRuns). Phase 3 then diffed against nil instead of last-cycle
state, causing the `if prev.CheckRuns != nil` guard in diff.go to
skip CI event detection entirely — silently missing transitions.

Fix: the UPDATE now includes `AND snapshot = '{}'` so it only fires
for items whose snapshot is still the UpsertTrackedItem default.
Already-tracked items with real snapshot data from a prior refresh
are left untouched. No extra query — the WHERE clause does the work
in the same UPDATE statement.

This preserves the original intent (Phase 2 classification sees
terminal state for newly-discovered backfill PRs) without regressing
the diffing path for items that have been tracked across multiple
poll cycles.

* fix(tracker): add repo queries only when we, yk, have repos

commit.message:
refactor: extract execution into ActionRunner layer (#95)

## Phase 8: Per-task factory methods with source-generated dispatch

### Summary

Replaces the monolithic `ActionRunner` (which held all execution
dependencies and used a hardcoded switch) with a source-generated
dispatch layer. Each task class now has a `FromTaskSpecification`
factory method, and the source generator auto-generates the
`ActionRunner` dispatch from existing `[CnctActionType]`
attributes on spec classes.

### Design

Each existing task class (LinkTask, CopyTask, etc.) is made
`partial` and gains a static `FromTaskSpecification` factory
method. This method constructs the task from its specification,
creating production-default dependencies internally.

The source generator (`CnctTaskSpecificationGenerator`) is
extended to produce:
1. Per-task partial declarations with `: CnctTaskBase`
   inheritance (hand-written parts omit the base class)
2. `ActionRunner.g.cs` with a dispatch switch that calls each
   task's factory method, derived by convention: strip
   "Specification" from spec class name to get task class name

`ActionRunner` is now a zero-dependency partial class that
delegates to the generated `DispatchAsync` method.

### Changes

**New files:**
- `IActionRunner.cs` - interface: `ExecuteAsync(ICnctActionSpec,
ILogger, string)`
- `ActionRunner.cs` - thin partial calling generated dispatch
- `ShellTask.cs` - new task class wrapping `ShellInvokerFactory`
  (the only action type that had no corresponding task class)

**Task classes modified (made partial, added FromTaskSpecification):**
- `LinkTask`, `CopyTask`, `LinkExpandTask`,
  `CloneGitRepositoryTask`, `EnvironmentVariableTask`

**Source generator extended:**
- Generates `XxxTask.g.cs` partials with `: CnctTaskBase`
- Generates `ActionRunner.g.cs` dispatch switch

**Interface changes:**
- Removed `ExecuteAsync` from `ICnctActionSpec` and
  `CnctActionSpecBase`
- All six spec classes no longer have `ExecuteAsync` overrides
- Specs that need deps for **validation** retain their deps

**Wiring:**
- `CnctConfig` gains an `IActionRunner Runner` property
- `CnctCommandLine` constructs `ActionRunner` and sets it

**Tests:**
- `ActionRunnerTests` - single test for unsupported spec type
  (execution behavior covered by existing per-task tests)
- `CnctConfigTagFilteringTests` - verifies via mock
  `IActionRunner`
- 98 tests passing, zero warnings

---------

Add artifacts_download module and example for universal packages (#763)

* Add example for downloading universal packages

Addresses #330: Adds a complete example demonstrating how to download
universal packages from Azure DevOps Artifacts, plus an
artifacts_download module with the reusable protocol implementation.

The download protocol involves:

1. Service discovery via ResourceAreas API
2. Package metadata retrieval from the packages service
3. Blob URL resolution via the dedup service
4. Manifest download and parsing
5. Chunk download with xpress decompression

commit.message:
Core/archetype separation — the big refactor

Architecture: three-layer model
  Core plugin (squad-federated): pure plumbing — worktrees, signals,
  knowledge flow, monitoring, OTel, launch. Knows nothing about what
  teams do.

  Archetype plugins: goal-specific behavior — prompt templates, playbooks,
  completion criteria, cleanup hooks. Installable separately. Per-team.

  User project: domain-specific skills, schemas, data.

Core changes (squad-federated):
- launch.ts: stripped ALL prompt builders. 4-tier prompt resolution:
  --prompt flag → --prompt-file → .squad/launch-prompt.md → generic fallback
- clearArtifacts → resetTeam(): only clears core state (signals).
  Runs .squad/cleanup-hook.sh if archetype provides one.
- Config stripped to 3 fields: branchPrefix, mcpStack, telemetry
- aggregate.ts removed from core (moved to deliverable archetype)

New: squad-archetype-deliverable plugin
- Scatter-gather pattern for producing file artifacts
- Playbook skill: discovery → analysis → deep-dives → validation → distillation
- 3 prompt templates (first/refresh/reset) with {team} placeholders
- cleanup-hook.sh: deletes deliverable + raw/
- aggregate.ts: collects deliverables from all team worktrees

New: squad-archetype-coding plugin
- Implementation teams that produce pull requests
- Playbook skill: design → implement → test → PR
- 3 prompt templates
- cleanup-hook.sh: no-op (code lives in git)

Marketplace: updated with both archetype plugins

commit.message:
fix: resolve deploy build failures from codename PR

Fix three issues introduced by the build-labeling PR (#258):

1. Android/iOS Fastfile: Replace `return` with `next` in private
   lanes. Ruby blocks (which Fastlane lanes are) raise
   `LocalJumpError: unexpected return` when `return` is used;
   `next` is the correct way to exit early from a block.

2. deploy-testflight.yml: Pass build metadata via environment
   variables instead of inline ${{ }} interpolation into JS
   single-quoted strings. Codenames containing apostrophes
   (e.g. "Allen's Swamp Monkey") break the JS parser with
   `SyntaxError: Unexpected identifier`.

fix: use _ensure_keys for generate_password; raise KeePassXCError for get_database_entries (#4)

generate-password does not require test-associate — only change-public-keys
needs to have been done so KeePassXC can encrypt the response. Calling
_ensure_session() caused failures when no association was stored even though
no association is actually needed for password generation.

get_database_entries now raises KeePassXCError with a helpful message instead
of silently returning an empty list when KeePassXC denies the request. The
action requires 'Allow access to all entries' to be enabled in KeePassXC
Settings → Browser Integration.

commit.message:
docs: add M1.2 Bulk Member Import implementation plan

Comprehensive implementation plan for M1.2 Bulk Member Import feature,
reviewed by Claude Opus 4.6, Claude Sonnet 4.6, and GPT 5.4.

Key design decisions:
- CSV-only for MVP (XLSX deferred to Phase 1b)
- REJECT + SKIP duplicate modes (MERGE deferred - underspecified)
- Spring @Async with bounded ThreadPoolTaskExecutor + TaskDecorator
- Bulk duplicate pre-loading via Set for O(1) per-row lookups
- Photo-free member creation path (BulkCreateMemberHelper)
- ImportInputAdapter wraps MemberInputNormalizer for batch safety
- PII masking in all failure responses
- SHA-256 file hash for idempotency collision detection
- Atomic SQL counter updates for progress tracking

Covers: API design (4 endpoints), database schema (V012),
domain model, async processing architecture, testing strategy,
15-task build sequence, and risk mitigations.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
